description_long: This object is a sterile adhesive bandage, commonly known as a band-aid, featuring a rectangular shape with rounded ends and a slightly raised central pad. The bandage is crossed in an 'X' formation, suggesting two individual strips overlapped at their centers. The surface is a pale, almost white color with a subtle woven or mesh texture, typical of fabric or flexible plastic bandages. The central area, where the strips overlap, is likely to contain a small absorbent pad for wound coverage, though it is not distinctly visible. The bandage appears thin and lightweight, with no visible branding, text, or decorative graphics. Its design is utilitarian, intended for single-use application to minor cuts or abrasions.
description: A pale, X-shaped adhesive bandage with a subtle woven texture and a thin, lightweight appearance. The overlapping strips suggest a sterile, utilitarian design for wound coverage.
description_view_0: The bandage is seen from above, showing the full X-shaped overlap of two strips with a faint woven texture.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the bandage's extreme thinness and flatness, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this top-down angle shows the X-shaped overlap and subtle surface texture.
description_view_3: Another side profile, nearly identical to view 1, emphasizing the object's thin, flat construction.
attribute_packaging_design: No packaging is visible; only the bare, crossed bandage strips are shown, with a plain, pale surface and no graphics or text.
attribute_adhesive_strip_pattern: The adhesive area has a subtle woven or mesh texture, with no visible color differences or printed markings.
